module.name: src
module.purpose: Rust workspace source root for Marix crates with flat Cargo crate layout. It organizes reusable common config/external re-export/logging/protocol/structure modules (config now includes a ToolConfig tool directory setting; protocol now owns the ToolSchema and ToolPreview tool metadata contracts), agent model/session/task orchestration, client session/event surfaces, host-side tool execution whose Executor::new(agent_tx) stores the shared agent SessionMessage sender and builds an implemented ToolRegistry that loads Tool handles from the configured tool directory (advertising ToolPreviews), and whose single route_session_event router intercepts Evoke to create and track a per-execution runtime running the host-local Tool handle (host/executor/tool.rs) and forwards other execution events to the target runtime, while the top-level tool crate (marix-tool) provides the ToolProgram interface and compiles one executable per native tool that the host's Tool handle spawns by filesystem path; the Executor-side Tool handle now lives in host/executor and host no longer depends on the tool crate.     The Cargo workspace members are common, agent, client, host, and tool, and the Cargo manifests define crate wiring. The agent, host, and client crates now each ship an executable entry point in addition to their [lib]: the marix-agent binary loads Config and runs Session::new(config.name) when config.agent.enabled, the marix-host binary runs HostSession::new(config.name), and the marix-client binary connects a ClientSession::new(config.name) and runs one-shot or interactive prompts; between-session (network) channels now carry a SessionMessage envelope (timestamp + source_name + wrapped SessionEvent): each session stores its Config.name in a module-level SOURCE_NAME static and exposes a static package_message(SessionEvent) -> SessionMessage so senders package events at the network boundary and receivers unwrap message.event, while internal per-Task and per-Execution control channels remain SessionEvent; every native tool program in the marix-tool crate now has an implemented invoke() that performs real filesystem/shell IO and returns JSON results (no longer panic stubs).

childModules.name: agent
childModules.purpose: Agent crate namespace for model backend abstractions, Session and Task runtime orchestration, and plain SessionState/TaskState storage. The model backend abstraction exposes only a send interface bridged by a blanket impl and includes an implemented Deepseek streaming backend; the session and task runtimes were refactored so worker/routing/step logic lives in private impl Session and private impl Task blocks in session.rs and task.rs. Session::new(name) stores the session name in a SOURCE_NAME static, spawns Self::spawn_client_worker(Arc::clone(&state)) and Self::spawn_host_worker(Arc::clone(&state)), exposes package_message(SessionEvent) -> SessionMessage, and its accept workers accept_channel::<SessionMessage> and unwrap message.event. Task::new spawns Self::event_loop(state, task_rx), raise() calls Self::run_step(Arc::clone(&self.state), step), and send_event packages each SessionEvent via Session::package_message before try_send to the client; the local task_tx/task_rx stay Sender/Receiver<SessionEvent>. session_state.rs and task_state.rs hold only their data structs plus new(). The crate now also ships the marix-agent binary entry (main.rs) that loads the global Config and, unless config.agent.enabled is false, runs Session::new(config.name) and parks the process.

childModules.name: client
childModules.purpose: Client crate namespace for the user-facing ClientSession and ClientEvent surface. ClientSession is now implemented as a self-connecting client that mirrors HostSession: new(name) stores the session name in a SOURCE_NAME static, constructs it and immediately spawns a worker (moving the user_tx channel half into it) that loads config.client.core_address, connect_channel::<SessionMessage> to the agent (retrying), stores the NetSender into the shared agent_tx, and runs a current-thread tokio runtime that unwraps each inbound agent SessionMessage and translates message.event into user-facing ClientEvents (content-bearing task events only, via to_client_event); is_connected() reports whether the worker has established the shared agent_tx NetSender, and the marix-client binary polls it in a bounded wait after new() so a create_task/cancel_task is not issued before that sender exists; create_task and cancel_task package task commands via the static package_message(SessionEvent) -> SessionMessage and send them to the agent through the shared agent_tx (SharedNetSender<SessionMessage>), receiver() borrows the ClientEvent stream (&Receiver<ClientEvent> over user_rx) for the caller to consume, and close() signals the shutdown flag and stops the worker. query_task was removed, the previously public user_rx field is now private, and the user_tx sender half is no longer a struct field (new() moves it into the worker); marix-client now depends on tokio (net, rt-multi-thread, sync, time) for the recv runtime. The crate now also ships the marix-client binary entry (main.rs) that loads Config, constructs a ClientSession::new(config.name) and waits via is_connected() until it is ready, and runs one-shot or interactive prompts, draining ClientEvent::Common messages until idle.

childModules.name: common
childModules.purpose: Shared common crate namespace with reusable config (now including a ToolConfig tool directory setting resolved to an absolute path at load time and a top-level name field identifying each session), now-public crate-local external re-export adapters that let downstream crates route third-party access through common, logging, protocol models including the ToolSchema and ToolPreview tool metadata contracts and the SessionMessage between-session envelope (timestamp + source_name + wrapped SessionEvent), and structural data/channel interfaces. Its protocol module now groups the step contracts (StepKind, StepSignature, StepEvent, StepResult, StepStatus, StepPreview) in a dedicated step child module, and the execution contracts (ExecutionEvent, ExecutionRequest, ExecutionUpdate, ExecutionSignature, ExecutionStatus) in a dedicated execution child module.

childModules.name: host
childModules.purpose: Host crate namespace for HostSession and host-side tool execution. HostSession is now implemented: it connects to the agent's configured host bind address (spawn_worker -> connect_channel::<SessionMessage>), owns a single per-connection Executor built by Executor::new(agent_tx), spawns a worker thread that pumps inbound agent SessionMessage envelopes into Executor::route_session_event (after unwrapping message.event) over a current-thread tokio runtime, and closes by signaling an Arc<AtomicBool> shutdown flag and joining the worker; new(name) stores the session name in a SOURCE_NAME static and HostSession exposes a static package_message(SessionEvent) -> SessionMessage that the executor and execution runtimes use to wrap outbound events; host/Cargo.toml now depends on tokio (net, rt-multi-thread, sync, time) for that runtime. The executor folder module's Executor::new(agent_tx) stores the shared agent SessionMessage sender and builds a ToolRegistry (a name-keyed collection of host Tool handles whose new() reads the global Config and loads every tool from the configured tool directory, with RegistryError reporting duplicate registrations) plus ExecutionRuntime records in a WorkQueue keyed by ExeId, each backed by a shared ExecutionContext, and exposes preview (delegating to the registry) alongside a single route_session_event router that intercepts ExecutionEvent::Evoke into the private create_execution (resolving a tool via the registry, spawning an ExecutionRuntime tracked by ExeId, and reporting unknown tools back to the agent as ExecutionStatus::Failed via the private emit_failed helper, which packages the event via HostSession::package_message) and forwards every other execution event through the private forward_to_execution, which borrows the target ExecutionRuntime's control Sender via executions.with and sends it the reconstructed SessionEvent (the local per-execution control channel stays SessionEvent); the execution child module implements ExecutionRuntime, which runs a Tool (the host-local handle defined in host/executor/tool.rs) via ExecutionContext and streams ExecutionStatus/ExecutionUpdate to the agent by synchronously locking agent_tx (std::sync::Mutex), packaging each SessionEvent via HostSession::package_message, and calling try_send. The Executor-side Tool handle now lives in host/executor/tool.rs, so host owns it internally and no longer depends on a tool crate; the ToolProgram interface and the native tool executables it spawns are built by the separate top-level marix-tool crate. The crate now also ships the marix-host binary entry (main.rs) that loads Config and runs HostSession::new(config.name) to serve host-side tool execution.

childModules.name: tool
childModules.purpose: Top-level marix-tool crate (lib marix_tool) that defines the per-tool ToolProgram interface and compiles one executable per native tool program (read_file, write_file, list_directory, search_text, shell_execute), each selected at build time via a Cargo feature and sharing the tool_main.rs --preview/--run entry. The Executor-side Tool handle that launches these executables lives in the host crate (host/executor), not in this crate. Every native tool program's invoke() is now implemented, parsing the JSON call and performing real filesystem/shell IO to return JSON results (no longer panic stubs).
